science.title: DAMPE satellite reveals cosmic rays share spectral break near 15 teravolts
science.summary: A century after their discovery, cosmic rays—particles of extreme energy originating from the far reaches of the universe—remain a mystery to scientists. The DAMPE (Dark Matter Particle Explorer) space telescope is tackling this phenomenon, particularly investigating the role that dark matter may play in their formatio

science.title: Scientists catch antimatter “atom” acting like a wave for the first time
science.summary: Quantum physics once shocked scientists by revealing that particles can behave like waves—and now, that strange behavior has been pushed even further. For the first time, researchers have observed wave-like interference in positronium, an exotic “atom” made of an electron and its antimatter partner, a positron. This br

science.title: Chemists unlock two-step alkene alkylation using stable acids and polar coupling
science.summary: Chemists at the Max-Planck-Institut für Kohlenforschung have developed a practical two-step method for alkylating alkenes via thianthrenation, addressing a long-standing synthetic challenge. This breakthrough simplifies complex molecule synthesis with applications in drug discovery, agrochemicals, and materials science

science.title: Harvard Breakthrough Brings Powerful UV Light Sources Onto a Chip
science.summary: Scientists have achieved a major step toward chip-scale ultraviolet light by converting red light into powerful UV within a tiny photonic device. Ultraviolet light, beyond what comes naturally from the sun, plays a central role in modern technology, including sterilization, biological imaging, and chip manufacturing. R

science.title: Scientists Make Breakthrough in Turning Plastic Trash Into Clean Fuel Using Sunlight
science.summary: Scientists are exploring a method to convert plastic waste into fuels and valuable chemicals using sunlight, potentially addressing both pollution and energy challenges. tech.title: The Download: DeepSeek’s latest AI breakthrough, and the race to build world models
tech.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. tech.title: Apple’s iPhone revenue jumps to $57 billion despite chip shortages
tech.summary: Apple's iPhone revenue jumped 22 percent to $57 billion over the past few months, despite supply chain issues impacting the device's processor. In an interview with Reuters, Apple CEO Tim Cook said iPhone "demand was off the charts," but there's "a little less flexibility at the moment for getting more parts." Cook add

tech.title: Uber taps Hertz to clean, charge, and fix its Lucid Motors robotaxis
tech.summary: Hertz is creating a new affiliate company called "Oro Mobility" to provide fleet management solutions "across a range of mobility segments."

tech.title: Dental practice software maker fixes bug that exposed patients’ medical records
tech.summary: The security bug is now fixed, but the patient who found it said it was challenging to alert the software company about the issue.

tech.title: Microsoft’s Xbox mode is now available for all Windows 11 PCs
tech.summary: Microsoft is now rolling out its Xbox mode to all Windows 11 PCs. The new Xbox mode adds a full-screen interface to the Xbox PC app, much like Steam's Big Picture Mode, and originally debuted as the Xbox Full Screen Experience (FSE) with Asus' Xbox Ally devices. "Some players in select markets will be able […]

medicine.title: Novel theranostic approach for radioimmunotherapy achieves curative responses in colorectal cancer tumors
medicine.summary: A new pretargeted radioimmunotherapy (PRIT) technique has been shown to be safe and effective in eradicating tumors from a preclinical colorectal cancer model. The multi-step theranostic approach delivers alpha-emitting radiation directly to tumors while limiting exposure to healthy tissues. This research is published

medicine.title: A forgotten drug is giving new hope to kids with a rare disease
medicine.summary: A decades-old drug once used to treat sleeping sickness is now showing surprising promise against an ultra-rare and life-threatening genetic disorder called Bachmann-Bupp syndrome (BABS). Early patient treatments suggest the drug, DFMO, may ease severe symptoms by targeting the underlying genetic malfunction. Researche

medicine.title: Simple “gut reset” may stop weight gain after Ozempic or Wegovy
medicine.summary: A new minimally invasive procedure may help people keep weight off after stopping popular drugs like Ozempic and semaglutide—something most patients struggle with. In a clinical trial, those who underwent a technique called duodenal mucosal resurfacing regained far less weight compared to others after discontinuing the

medicine.title: Hidden virus in your gut may be linked to colon cancer
medicine.summary: A newly discovered virus hiding inside a common gut bacterium could help explain one of medicine’s long-standing mysteries: why a microbe found in both healthy people and cancer patients is linked to colorectal cancer. The research suggests that the interaction between bacteria and the viruses they carry may be key to

medicine.title: Two common drugs may reverse fatty liver disease, study finds
medicine.summary: Scientists have discovered that combining two existing drugs can dramatically reduce liver fat linked to a common and often silent disease. The treatment not only improved liver health in animal models but also showed potential to lower heart-related risks. Interestingly, using lower doses of both drugs together worked

mathematics.title: In Math, Rigor Is Vital. But Are Digitized Proofs Taking It Too Far?
mathematics.summary: The quest to make mathematics rigorous has a long and spotty history — one mathematicians can learn from as they push to formalize everything in the computer program Lean. The post In Math, Rigor Is Vital. But Are Digitized Proofs Taking It Too Far? first appeared on Quanta Magazine

mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win AI for Math grants to accelerate mathematical discovery
mathematics.summary: Department of Mathematics researchers David Roe and Andrew Sutherland seek to advance automated theorem proving; four additional MIT alumni also awarded.

mathematics.title: School of Science welcomed new faculty in 2024
mathematics.summary: Eleven new professors join the departments of Biology; Brain and Cognitive Sciences; Earth, Atmospheric and Planetary Sciences; Mathematics; and Physics.

mathematics.title: Laurent Demanet appointed co-director of MIT Center for Computational Science and Engineering
mathematics.summary: Applied mathematics professor will join fellow co-director Nicolas Hadjiconstantinou in leading the cross-cutting center.

mathematics.title: Why Math’s Final Axiom Proved So Controversial
mathematics.summary: Zermelo-Fraenkel set theory is so widely accepted that modern mathematicians hardly think about it. But believing in its core principles didn’t come easily. The post Why Math’s Final Axiom Proved So Controversial first appeared on Quanta Magazine

space.title: SpaceX launches its 50th mission of the year, sends 25 Starlink satellites to orbit (video)
space.summary: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket launched 25 Starlink internet satellites to orbit from California on Sunday (April 26). It was the company's 50th liftoff of the year already.

space.title: SpaceX rocket debris could slam into the moon: Here's what you need to know
space.summary: Earth's moon is to be on the receiving end of a spent rocket stage in early August - the leftovers from a SpaceX Falcon 9 launch last year.

space.title: This SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket launch looks amazing from space in these wild satellite photos
space.summary: SpaceX's Falcon Heavy rocket flew for the first time in 18 months Wednesday (April 29), and a sharp-eyed satellite was watching.

space.title: Welcome home! Artemis 2's Orion capsule returns to Florida after epic moon mission (photo)
space.summary: Artemis 2's Orion capsule has returned to its Florida launch site, just three weeks after carrying four astronauts on a historic journey around the moon's far side.

space.title: 'This is going to be what makes the Earth secure.' How one California company plans to protect us from dangerous asteroids
space.summary: Exploration Labs has proposed the first commercial deep space ride share mission, known as Apophis EX, to rendezvous with potentially hazardous asteroid Apophis.

astronomy.title: What is the Most Common Type of Planet in the Galaxy?
astronomy.summary: Astronomers now believe there is at least one planet for every star in the Milky Way but new research has revealed a deeply unsettling twist in that picture. astronomy.title: Binary Stars Form Lots Of Exoplanets, But Many Of Them Are Ejected As Rogue Planets
astronomy.summary: Binary stars are common, but for a long time astronomers have thought that exoplanets would have trouble forming around them. astronomy.title: Interstellar Comet 3I/ATLAS Has Cold, Ancient Origins
astronomy.summary: The most recent interstellar visitor was crisscrossing our galaxy for some 10 to 12 billion years before it came near the Sun. The post Interstellar Comet 3I/ATLAS Has Cold, Ancient Origins appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: May Podcast: The Delightful Dippers
astronomy.summary: This month’s episode showcases the Big and Little Dippers, now placed high in the northern evening sky. We'll also track down all five bright planets and watch for meteors from Halley's Comet. So grab your curiosity, and come along on this month’s Sky Tour. astronomy.title: Catching the Earliest Stars in the Universe
astronomy.summary: New observations from the James Webb Space Telescope might have caught the signature of the universe's first stars. The post Catching the Earliest Stars in the Universe appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

architecture.title: SOM "preserves the soul" of 1960s modernist office complex in Milan
architecture.summary: Global studio SOM has renewed Corso Italia 23, a 1960s office complex in Milan, retrofitting its modernist structure and fractured interiors to align with modern standards. architecture.title: Diamond Schmitt and MVRDV reveal design of terraced research building for University of Toronto
architecture.summary: Architecture studios Diamond Schmitt, MVRDV and Two Row Architect have revealed the design for the Temerty Building at the University of Toronto, a research building informed by Indigenous design principles. The building will be located on the edge of a campus lawn at the University of Toronto St George campus and host

architecture.title: Gensler set to design Trump Tower Tbilisi as country's tallest building
architecture.summary: The Trump Organization has announced plans to build a 70-storey skyscraper in Tbilisi, Georgia, which would be the tallest building in the country. architecture.title: zU-studio installs sunflower sculptural bench into san sebastián’s urban plaza
architecture.summary: zU-studio embeds traditional basque symbols into the spatial design. The post zU-studio installs sunflower sculptural bench into san sebastián’s urban plaza appeared first on designboom | architecture & design magazine.

architecture.title: three-toned marble facades clad floating kiosk in beijing’s urban fabric
architecture.summary: a triangular volume defines the overall architectural form, while a circular opening disrupts the continuity of the mass. The post three-toned marble facades clad floating kiosk in beijing’s urban fabric appeared first on designboom | architecture & design magazine.

construction.title: ‘Unique engineering challenge’ as mid-century bridge is lifted clear of River Hull
construction.summary: The 73-year-old Weel Bridge in East Yorkshire has been removed by Esh Construction, marking a key stage in its £1M refurbishment. The post ‘Unique engineering challenge’ as mid-century bridge is lifted clear of River Hull appeared first on New Civil Engineer.

construction.title: Granite scoops up Utah infrastructure contractor
construction.summary: The California-based firm bought Kenny Seng Construction, which focuses on earthwork and site preparation as well as managing a gravel pit and recycling yard.

construction.title: Kiewit ‘off-ramped’ from Baltimore’s Key Bridge rebuild
construction.summary: The Maryland Transportation Authority informed the firm Tuesday that it would not be retained for the next phase of the project’s construction, which has seen its cost balloon.

construction.title: Letter to the Editor: There's Nothing New Under the Sun for Bridges

ai.title: OpenAI models, Codex, and Managed Agents come to AWS
ai.summary: OpenAI GPT models, Codex, and Managed Agents are now available on AWS, enabling enterprises to build secure AI in their AWS environments.
